story_titles: Beavis Ultrasound PnP ISA Sound Card Replica
Cyberpunk Comics, Manga and Graphic Novels
full_text: Eric "Beavis" Schlae released an open-source replica of the Gravis Ultrasound PnP, a landmark ISA sound card from the 1990s. The original Gravis Ultrasound was popular among DOS gamers and music software users for its wavetable synthesis and CD-quality audio. The Beavis Ultrasound PnP has a schematic and reverse-engineered source code that uses an AMD InterWave chip to build. It needs this chip to work. So you're thinking of building a functional sound card - just keep in mind the design hasn't been tested, so you're taking on that risk yourself. Build at your own risk. Explore the roots of cyberpunk media with "The Long Tomorrow", a 1975 graphic novel. "The Long Tomorrow" by Dan O'Bannon and Moebius predicts a heavily urbanized future society, inspiring films like Blade Runner. This graphic novel is like a window into cyberpunk's early days. It's worth checking out if you're into the genre. Give it a look. For retro computer enthusiasts, check out Tiny Emulators, a collection of emulators for retro computers and consoles. Tiny Emulators has KC85 and Amstrad CPC464 emulators with games like Boulderdash. They're on GitHub, Twitter, and a blog, with UIs for each. Check them out. Follow for daily tech news
